title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 103.
content:
There was an Old Lady of Prague,
    Whose language was horribly vague;
    When they said, "Are these caps?"
    She answered, "Perhaps!"
    That oracular Lady of Prague.
meaning: About: Edward Lear was an English artist, illustrator and writer known for his literary nonsense, in poetry and prose, and especially his limericks, a form which he popularised.
